content:
Through these symbols, Welty reveals Phoenix Jackson as a figure of perseverance and sacrifice, embodying the strength required to face hardships. Her symbolic connection to the phoenix and the worn path she traverses highlight her unyielding dedication to her mission, reinforcing the themes of perseverance and sacrifice in the narrative.

from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 7, 2025 8:57PM
ZIFENG WU
Hi Michael,

Your analysis of Phoenix Jackson’s symbolic connection to the mythological phoenix and the worn path is insightful. I particularly appreciate your interpretation of the path as a symbol not only of her physical journey but also of the broader struggles she faces in life. The path’s description as “a thin, black, crooked thread” really emphasizes the unpredictable, often painful nature of her journey, yet Phoenix continues with unwavering persistence.

Additionally, I think it’s worth highlighting how Welty uses Phoenix’s encounter with the hunters to further explore the theme of perseverance. When they mock her, Phoenix’s calm response and her continued focus on her mission illustrate her ability to rise above societal obstacles. As she tells them, “I seen plenty go off to the graveyard,” she acknowledges life’s hardships without letting them deter her from her purpose. This reinforces the idea that Phoenix’s strength is not just physical, but deeply rooted in her spirit and resilience.
